assumption_text: The Marjah estimate uses the Marjah–Nad Ali clear-phase footprint, not all of central Helmand and not the entire outer support belt implied by the 15,000-force figure.
rationale: The broader 15,000 number clearly covers a larger operational area than the directly contested Marjah–Nad Ali theater used for civilian exposure.

assumption_text: Average civilian presence is scenario-based at 40,000 / 60,000 / 80,000 over the 18-day clear phase.
rationale: Available reporting gives a pre-offensive population (~80,000), a remaining-population floor (>40,000), and combined Marjah-Nad Ali displacement figures but not a daily curve.

assumption_text: The military-deaths estimate for Moshtarak likely undercounts Afghan-security fatalities because no episode-specific ANSF death series was found.
rationale: The available narrow-window source directly reports NATO deaths and Taliban deaths but not Afghan-force deaths.

assumption_text: The Kunduz denominator is matched to a city-plus-periphery theater rather than city-only because the strongest civilian numerator is province-wide for a city-centered battle.
rationale: Using city-only population against a province-wide civilian numerator would artificially inflate civilian death rates.

assumption_text: Military exposure is bracketed between lower actual-fighter counts and higher nominal garrison counts.
rationale: Reuters reports ~6,000 defenders, while Malkasian suggests roughly 3,000 government-side fighters actually mattered in the collapse, plus about 500 Taliban attackers.

assumption_text: The 80 / 150 / 200 organized-armed death range is anchored on the official 150 Taliban-killed claim but widened to reflect both possible inflation and missing additional episode fatalities.
rationale: No single source cleanly enumerates all organized-armed fatalities over the full 16-day window.

assumption_text: The 100,000-fled figure is treated as a later-episode bound rather than an immediate day-one population drop.
rationale: The Reuters displacement figure is dated after the episode window and does not specify the daily outflow pattern.

assumption_text: The Abbey Gate estimate covers the gate corridor and adjacent queueing approaches on Aug. 26, not the entire airport or all of Kabul.
rationale: A broader denominator would dilute the event-specific civilian death rate and violate the theater rule.

assumption_text: Airport-wide U.S. and Afghan security counts are used as a proxy for directly exposed armed personnel because gate-specific rosters are unavailable.
rationale: No source found a precise Abbey Gate-only troop count for Aug. 26.

assumption_text: Civilian exposure at Abbey Gate is model-generated from throughput and crowding context rather than directly observed headcounts.
rationale: Available sources describe thousands at the gates and high throughput but do not provide a simultaneous gate-corridor census.

assumption_text: Pre-bombing crush and mixed-cause airport deaths are excluded from the bombing DR core.
rationale: They belong to different dates and mixed cause/status categories and would contaminate the one-day bombing numerator.

assumption_text: Episode durations are converted to person-months using 30-day months.
rationale: This standardizes short-episode exposure calculations across all episodes.
